page: Amazon Web Services dl2q.24xlarge is a specialized instance designed for deep learning workloads, featuring Qualcomm inference accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this server is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. The system includes 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps and has no local storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, showing strong results in both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads and writes. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the middle 50%, while single-threaded gzip compression performance is in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dl2q.24xlarge offers a dense resource profile with high memory capacity and dedicated CPU allocation, making it suitable for memory-intensive operations without the overhead of local storage. The instance is optimized for deep learning inference, database operations, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services dl2q.24xlarge is a specialized deep learning inference instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and a 2.5 GHz clock speed, paired with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU capabilities and high memory bandwidth, alongside strong database and Redis SET operations. However, single-threaded performance remains average to weak. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-dense workloads and parallel processing tasks. It is highly suited for deep learning inference, large-scale database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services dl2q.24xlarge is a Nitro-based instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and Qualcomm inference accelerators, delivering strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services dl2q.24xlarge features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for deep learning inference workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed networking for parallelized inference and database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores
768.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core CPU, database, and Redis SET benchmark performance
Designed for deep learning inference and memory-intensive workloads
